commands.name: grep
commands.description: Search for lines matching a pattern in files

patterns.text: find all lines containing a word in a file
patterns.explanation: Searches logfile.txt for lines containing the word 'error'

patterns.text: search for a string across all files in a directory recursively
patterns.explanation: Recursively searches all files under src/ for lines containing 'TODO'

patterns.text: search case insensitively for a pattern
patterns.explanation: Matches 'warning', 'WARNING', 'Warning', etc. in all .log files

patterns.text: show line numbers for matches
patterns.explanation: Displays matching lines with their line numbers

patterns.text: show only filenames that contain a match
patterns.explanation: Lists only the names of files that contain 'password'

patterns.text: find lines that do not match a pattern
patterns.explanation: Shows all lines that are not comments (don't start with #)

commands.name: sed
commands.description: Stream editor for filtering and transforming text

patterns.text: replace a word in a file
patterns.explanation: Replaces all occurrences of 'old' with 'new' and prints to stdout

patterns.text: edit a file in place replacing text
patterns.explanation: Modifies the file directly, replacing every 'foo' with 'bar'

patterns.text: delete lines matching a pattern
patterns.explanation: Removes all blank lines from the output

patterns.text: print only specific lines from a file
patterns.explanation: Prints only lines 10 through 20

patterns.text: insert a line before a matching line
patterns.explanation: Inserts 'new line of text' before every line matching 'pattern'

commands.name: awk
commands.description: Pattern scanning and text processing language

patterns.text: print a specific column from a file
patterns.explanation: Prints the second whitespace-delimited field of each line

patterns.text: sum up numbers in a column
patterns.explanation: Adds up all values in the first column and prints the total

patterns.text: filter rows where a column meets a condition
patterns.explanation: Prints lines where the third field is greater than 100

patterns.text: use a custom field separator like comma
patterns.explanation: Uses comma as delimiter and prints the first and third columns

patterns.text: count the number of lines in a file
patterns.explanation: Prints the total number of records (lines) processed

commands.name: cut
commands.description: Remove sections from each line of files

patterns.text: extract a specific column from a CSV
patterns.explanation: Uses comma as delimiter and extracts the second field

patterns.text: get the first 10 characters of each line
patterns.explanation: Extracts characters 1 through 10 from every line

patterns.text: extract multiple fields from a tab-separated file
patterns.explanation: Extracts fields 1, 3, and 5 using the default tab delimiter

patterns.text: get everything after the first field
patterns.explanation: Extracts from the second field to the end using colon as delimiter

patterns.text: extract a range of bytes from each line
patterns.explanation: Extracts bytes 1 through 5 from each line

commands.name: tr
commands.description: Translate or delete characters

patterns.text: convert lowercase to uppercase
patterns.explanation: Translates all lowercase letters to uppercase

patterns.text: delete specific characters from text
patterns.explanation: Removes all digits, leaving only letters

patterns.text: squeeze repeated characters into one
patterns.explanation: Collapses multiple consecutive spaces into a single space

patterns.text: replace newlines with spaces to make one line
patterns.explanation: Converts a multi-line file into a single line

patterns.text: replace tabs with spaces
patterns.explanation: Converts every tab character into a space

commands.name: sort
commands.description: Sort lines of text files

patterns.text: sort a file alphabetically
patterns.explanation: Sorts all lines in alphabetical order

patterns.text: sort numbers in numeric order
patterns.explanation: Sorts lines numerically rather than lexicographically

patterns.text: sort in reverse order
patterns.explanation: Sorts lines in descending order

patterns.text: sort by a specific column
patterns.explanation: Sorts a CSV by the second column using comma as the delimiter

patterns.text: remove duplicate lines while sorting
patterns.explanation: Sorts and removes duplicate lines in one step

commands.name: uniq
commands.description: Report or omit repeated lines

patterns.text: remove consecutive duplicate lines
patterns.explanation: Sorts first then removes duplicates since uniq only works on adjacent lines

patterns.text: count how many times each line appears
patterns.explanation: Prefixes each unique line with the number of occurrences

patterns.text: show only the lines that appear more than once
patterns.explanation: Prints only the duplicated lines

patterns.text: show only lines that are unique and never repeated
patterns.explanation: Prints only the lines that appear exactly once

patterns.text: find the most frequent lines in a file
patterns.explanation: Counts occurrences, sorts by frequency, and shows the top entries

commands.name: wc
commands.description: Print newline, word, and byte counts for files

patterns.text: count the number of lines in a file
patterns.explanation: Prints the line count of the file

patterns.text: count the number of words in a file
patterns.explanation: Prints the total word count

patterns.text: count the number of characters in a file
patterns.explanation: Prints the character count

patterns.text: get line counts for all files in a directory
patterns.explanation: Shows line count per Python file and a total

commands.name: head
commands.description: Output the first part of files

patterns.text: show the first 10 lines of a file
patterns.explanation: Displays the first 10 lines by default

patterns.text: show the first N lines of a file
patterns.explanation: Displays the first 20 lines

patterns.text: show everything except the last N lines
patterns.explanation: Prints all lines except the last 5

patterns.text: show the first few bytes of a file
patterns.explanation: Displays the first 100 bytes of the file

commands.name: tail
commands.description: Output the last part of files

patterns.text: show the last 10 lines of a file
patterns.explanation: Displays the last 10 lines by default

patterns.text: show the last N lines of a file
patterns.explanation: Displays the last 50 lines

patterns.text: follow a log file in real time as it grows
patterns.explanation: Continuously outputs new lines appended to the file

patterns.text: show everything starting from a specific line
patterns.explanation: Prints from line 100 to the end of the file

patterns.text: follow a log file and stop when process dies
patterns.explanation: Follows the log until the process with PID 1234 terminates

commands.name: tee
commands.description: Read from stdin and write to stdout and files simultaneously

patterns.text: save command output to a file while still seeing it
patterns.explanation: Writes the directory listing to both the screen and listing.txt

patterns.text: append output to a file instead of overwriting
patterns.explanation: Appends the text to log.txt rather than replacing its contents

patterns.text: write output to multiple files at once
patterns.explanation: Writes the same content to both copy1.txt and copy2.txt

patterns.text: pipe through tee to log intermediate results in a pipeline
patterns.explanation: Saves matching lines to errors.txt and also counts them

commands.name: paste
commands.description: Merge lines of files side by side

patterns.text: merge two files side by side with tabs
patterns.explanation: Combines corresponding lines from both files separated by tabs

patterns.text: merge files with a custom delimiter
patterns.explanation: Joins lines from both files using a comma as the separator

patterns.text: convert a column of data into a single comma-separated line
patterns.explanation: Joins all lines into one line separated by commas

patterns.text: arrange a single column into multiple columns
patterns.explanation: Arranges input into three columns by reading from stdin three times

commands.name: join
commands.description: Join lines of two sorted files on a common field

patterns.text: join two sorted files on the first column
patterns.explanation: Merges lines from both files that share a matching first field

patterns.text: join files on a different column
patterns.explanation: Joins on the second field of file1 and first field of file2

patterns.text: join with a custom delimiter
patterns.explanation: Uses comma as both input and output field separator

patterns.text: show unmatched lines from the first file
patterns.explanation: Includes lines from file1 that have no match in file2

commands.name: comm
commands.description: Compare two sorted files line by line

patterns.text: find lines unique to the first file
patterns.explanation: Shows only lines present in file1 but not file2

patterns.text: find lines common to both files
patterns.explanation: Shows only lines that appear in both files

patterns.text: find lines unique to the second file
patterns.explanation: Shows only lines present in file2 but not file1

patterns.text: compare two sorted files showing all three columns
patterns.explanation: Displays three columns: unique to file1, unique to file2, and common

commands.name: diff
commands.description: Compare files line by line and show differences

patterns.text: compare two files and show the differences
patterns.explanation: Shows lines that differ between the two files

patterns.text: show differences side by side
patterns.explanation: Displays both files in parallel with differences marked

patterns.text: generate a unified diff for patching
patterns.explanation: Creates a unified format diff suitable for the patch command

patterns.text: compare two directories recursively
patterns.explanation: Compares all corresponding files in both directory trees

patterns.text: ignore whitespace differences when comparing
patterns.explanation: Treats all sequences of whitespace as equal

commands.name: patch
commands.description: Apply a diff file to an original file

patterns.text: apply a patch file to source code
patterns.explanation: Applies the changes described in the patch file

patterns.text: apply a patch with a directory strip level
patterns.explanation: Strips one leading directory from paths in the patch

patterns.text: reverse a previously applied patch
patterns.explanation: Undoes the changes made by the patch

patterns.text: do a dry run to check if a patch applies cleanly
patterns.explanation: Tests whether the patch can be applied without actually modifying files

commands.name: column
commands.description: Format input into multiple columns or a table

patterns.text: format data as a nicely aligned table
patterns.explanation: Auto-aligns columns based on whitespace delimiters

patterns.text: format a CSV as a readable table
patterns.explanation: Uses comma as the input separator and aligns into columns

patterns.text: arrange a list into multiple columns for display
patterns.explanation: Arranges the input into columns to fill the terminal width

patterns.text: format colon-delimited data as a table
patterns.explanation: Displays the passwd file in a readable tabular format

commands.name: fold
commands.description: Wrap each input line to fit a specified width

patterns.text: wrap long lines to 80 characters
patterns.explanation: Breaks lines at 80 characters, possibly mid-word

patterns.text: wrap lines at word boundaries instead of mid-word
patterns.explanation: Breaks at the last space within 72 characters to avoid splitting words

patterns.text: limit output width to a specific number of bytes
patterns.explanation: Counts bytes instead of columns when determining line width

patterns.text: reformat a paragraph to a narrower width
patterns.explanation: Wraps the paragraph text to 60 characters at word boundaries

commands.name: fmt
commands.description: Simple text formatter that reflows paragraphs

patterns.text: reformat a text file to 75 character width
patterns.explanation: Reflows paragraphs so lines are at most 75 characters wide

patterns.text: reflow a paragraph to standard width
patterns.explanation: Reformats paragraphs to the default width of approximately 75 characters

patterns.text: format text with uniform spacing between words
patterns.explanation: Ensures uniform spacing of one space between words and two after sentences

patterns.text: format text for a narrow terminal
patterns.explanation: Reflows text to fit within 40 columns

commands.name: expand
commands.description: Convert tabs to spaces

patterns.text: convert all tabs to spaces in a file
patterns.explanation: Replaces each tab with the appropriate number of spaces to reach the next tab stop

patterns.text: convert tabs using a custom tab width
patterns.explanation: Uses a tab width of 4 spaces instead of the default 8

patterns.text: convert only leading tabs to spaces
patterns.explanation: Replaces only tabs at the beginning of lines, leaving others intact

patterns.text: standardize indentation from tabs to 2 spaces
patterns.explanation: Converts every tab to 2 spaces for consistent indentation

commands.name: unexpand
commands.description: Convert spaces to tabs

patterns.text: convert leading spaces back to tabs
patterns.explanation: Replaces leading spaces with tabs based on the default tab stop of 8

patterns.text: convert all spaces to tabs not just leading
patterns.explanation: Converts all sequences of spaces (not just leading) to tabs

patterns.text: convert spaces to tabs with a custom tab width
patterns.explanation: Uses a tab stop of 4 when converting spaces to tabs

patterns.text: change indentation from 2 spaces to tabs
patterns.explanation: Converts leading 2-space indentation to tabs

commands.name: nl
commands.description: Number lines of files

patterns.text: add line numbers to a file
patterns.explanation: Numbers non-empty lines and prints the file with line numbers

patterns.text: number all lines including blank ones
patterns.explanation: Assigns a number to every line, even blank lines

patterns.text: number lines with a custom format
patterns.explanation: Uses right-justified zero-padded 4-digit line numbers

patterns.text: number lines starting from a specific number
patterns.explanation: Starts numbering from 100 instead of 1

commands.name: rev
commands.description: Reverse characters in each line

patterns.text: reverse each line of a file
patterns.explanation: Reverses the character order of every line

patterns.text: reverse a string
patterns.explanation: Outputs 'dlrow olleh'

patterns.text: get the last field when you don't know how many fields there are
patterns.explanation: Reverses, takes the first field, then reverses again to extract the last field

patterns.text: check if a string is a palindrome
patterns.explanation: Compares the string with its reverse to check for palindrome

commands.name: tac
commands.description: Concatenate and print files in reverse line order

patterns.text: print a file with lines in reverse order
patterns.explanation: Prints the last line first and the first line last

patterns.text: reverse the order of log entries
patterns.explanation: Shows the 20 most recent log entries by reversing and taking the top

patterns.text: reverse paragraphs separated by blank lines
patterns.explanation: Reverses the order of paragraph blocks separated by empty lines

patterns.text: reverse lines from a pipe
patterns.explanation: Prints numbers 10 down to 1

commands.name: strings
commands.description: Find printable strings in binary files

patterns.text: extract readable text from a binary file
patterns.explanation: Finds and prints all sequences of printable characters in the binary

patterns.text: find strings longer than a minimum length in a binary
patterns.explanation: Shows only strings that are at least 10 characters long

patterns.text: search for URLs inside a binary file
patterns.explanation: Extracts text strings and filters for those containing 'http'

patterns.text: show the byte offset of each string found
patterns.explanation: Displays each string preceded by its hexadecimal offset in the file

commands.name: iconv
commands.description: Convert text between character encodings

patterns.text: convert a file from Latin-1 to UTF-8
patterns.explanation: Converts the file encoding from ISO-8859-1 (Latin-1) to UTF-8

patterns.text: list all available character encodings
patterns.explanation: Prints all supported encoding names

patterns.text: convert UTF-8 to ASCII and discard unconvertible characters
patterns.explanation: Transliterates characters that have no ASCII equivalent

patterns.text: convert a file from Windows encoding to UTF-8
patterns.explanation: Converts Windows-1252 encoded text to standard UTF-8

commands.name: dos2unix
commands.description: Convert line endings between DOS/Windows and Unix formats

patterns.text: convert Windows line endings to Unix
patterns.explanation: Removes carriage return characters (\r) from line endings

patterns.text: convert Unix line endings to Windows format
patterns.explanation: Adds carriage return (\r) before each newline

patterns.text: convert line endings and save to a different file
patterns.explanation: Converts line endings and writes to a new file, keeping the original intact

patterns.text: convert all files in a directory from DOS to Unix format
patterns.explanation: Recursively finds all .txt files and converts their line endings
